Customer Marketing

Thought Leadership & Executive Content

Executive ghostwriting, branded interview series, webinars, and thought leadership content that turn expertise into influence.

Book a strategy session

Influence in EdTech is earned through consistent, credible expertise — not a single viral post. We build owned thought-leadership programs the same way we built our own: The EdTech Mentor, our interview series, now reaches more than 6,500 subscribers and has featured over 80 practitioners across three series — Essentials, Founders, Investor. We bring that exact production model to client brands.

What this looks like

Editorial Strategy: Concept, Positioning & Framework

The strategic foundation for a thought-leadership or interview program — purpose, target audience, guest-profile criteria, thematic pillars, and how the brand positions itself as host or curator. Establishes the "why" before the "what."

Guest Sourcing & Interview Production

End-to-end production of one episode: a guest-specific questionnaire, remote HD recording, edited transcript, a full video, a website and LinkedIn article, two illustration concepts, a short animation, a teaser clip, and newsletter distribution.

Expert Interview / Core Content

A structured interview with a customer, prospect, or internal specialist, turned into a 1,500-word document that becomes the raw material for an entire content campaign — blog post, social posts, email, and newsletter.

Whitepaper / Research Content

Long-form, authoritative content (2,500–5,000 words) presenting original analysis or expert perspective on institutional pain points — delivered as a designed PDF, commonly used as a gated lead magnet.

Executive Ghostwriting

Branded, executive-voiced content — op-eds, LinkedIn thought pieces, and webinar scripting — that puts your leadership team's expertise in front of the audience that needs to hear it.

Proof point

WorldQuant Learning's 2026 thought-leadership program runs 12 interviews a year, each with full production: questionnaire, recording, transcript, video, article, two illustrations, animation, teaser, and newsletter distribution. It's the same model, scoped for a client brand instead of our own.

Part of Customer Marketing

Turn your team's expertise into an owned media channel.
